meta.title: Lev Effect Control for Fractal Roots
meta.description: A substrate-neutral design brief for governing agent side effects with EffectEnvelope, ObservedEffect, EffectReceipt, and substrate evaluators.

props.title: Effect Control for Fractal Roots
props.subtitle: Agent work is governed as proof-bearing effects, not just code diffs.
props.meta: draft design | updated 2026-05-25 | substrate-neutral semantic control

props.content: Lev should treat effect as the conceptual primitive, but expose it with Lev-owned names: EffectEnvelope, ObservedEffect, EffectReceipt, and EffectPolicy. Boundary remains real, but only as the code/SDLC evaluator code_boundary.

props.label: Core Move
props.title: One Envelope, Many Substrates
props.subtitle: The same control loop governs code, documents, browser actions, shell commands, graph mutations, and timelines.

trend.value: code, docs, browser, shell, cloud, data, message, video, graph, FlowMind

props.label: Correction
props.title: Boundary Was Too Narrow for the Universal Layer
props.subtitle: The prior design used a code word as a system word. This version separates them.

props.title: Old framing
props.content: Boundary as the universal primitive. This fits code but fails for Google Drive documents, browser sessions, cloud resources, messages, and video timelines.

props.title: Corrected framing
props.content: EffectEnvelope is universal. code_boundary is one substrate evaluator. document_scope, web_effect_policy, resource_effect_policy, and graph_projection_integrity are siblings.

props.label: Runtime Model
props.title: Effect-Control Loop
props.subtitle: The proof-bearing part still happens after ACT, when the observed effect exists.

props.title: Intent
props.content: Seed intent, constraints, actor, and active FlowMind context.

props.title: Envelope
props.content: Declare substrate, target, operation, scope, evidence, and evaluators.

props.title: Admission
props.content: Check authority, effect scope, budget, and tool readiness before dispatch.

props.title: ACT
props.content: Run the substrate adapter: code edit, doc revision, browser action, shell command, or graph op.

props.title: Verify
props.content: Extract ObservedEffect and evaluate intent, scope, evidence, drift, and substrate policy.

props.title: Receipt
props.content: Record keep, hold, mutate, discard, or escalate in EffectReceipt and proof_of_effect_control.

props.label: Contract
props.title: EffectEnvelope Shape
props.subtitle: The envelope is substrate-neutral. Evaluators specialize it.

props.content:
effect_envelope:
  id: effect-uuid
  actor: agent-session-id
  substrate: code | document | browser | shell | database | cloud | message | timeline | graph | flowmind
  target:
    uri: substrate-specific-uri
    revision: optional-revision-or-version
  operation: create | read | update | delete | execute | send | share | export | navigate | apply
  intended_effect: string
  allowed_scope: {}
  constraints: []
  required_evidence: []
  evaluators:
    - intent_conservation
    - effect_scope
    - authority_policy
    - evidence_sufficiency
    - semantic_drift
    - substrate_specific_evaluator

props.caption: Core FlowMind effect control. Substrate overlays add evaluators, not replacement loops.
props.content:
flowchart TD
  A[Intent] --> B[EffectEnvelope]
  B --> C{Admission Gate}
  C -->|admit| D[ACT through substrate adapter]
  C -->|hold| H[Hold / ask / repair]
  D --> E[ObservedEffect]
  E --> F{Verify Gate}
  F -->|keep| G[EffectReceipt]
  F -->|unclear| H
  F -->|mutate| B
  F -->|discard| I[Discard / escalate]
  G --> J[Graph / receipt / proof update]

props.label: Substrates
props.title: What the Evaluator Means by Surface
props.subtitle: Every substrate has a different scope model. That is why code boundary cannot be the universal term.

props.label: Example
props.title: Google Drive Word Doc Is Not a Code Boundary
props.subtitle: The same effect envelope works, but the evaluator changes.

props.content:
effect_envelope:
  substrate: document
  target:
    uri: gdrive://document/<doc-id>
    revision: rev-before
  operation: update
  intended_effect: Rewrite the executive summary for clarity.
  allowed_scope:
    sections: [Executive Summary]
    forbidden_operations: [share, delete, export, rename, permission_update]
  constraints:
    - preserve citations
    - preserve comments unless explicitly resolved
    - preserve document style template
  required_evidence: [revision_diff, changed_ranges, final_revision_id]
  evaluators:
    - intent_conservation
    - document_scope
    - authority_policy
    - evidence_sufficiency

props.title: Document evaluator asks
props.content: Did the agent edit the intended document id and revision? Did it touch only the allowed section or range? Did it preserve citations, comments, tone, style, and sharing policy? Does the revision diff prove the requested change?

props.label: Prior Art
props.title: TypeScript Effect Is Useful, but Already Owns the Name
props.subtitle: The research lane found a mature upstream vocabulary that Lev should respect.

props.title: Effect<A, E, R>
props.content: Upstream Effect models a lazy typed computation with success, expected error, and requirements. Lev should not export a bare competing Effect type.

props.title: Context and Layer
props.content: Effect uses typed dependency environments. Lev can borrow the shape by mapping Lev dependencies to Context/Layer if the library is adopted.

props.title: Cause and Scope
props.content: Effect keeps rich failure causes and resource lifetimes. Lev should mirror this in receipts and substrate scopes, not confuse it with the envelope itself.

props.title: Runtime and Fibers
props.content: Effect runs computations on fibers through Runtime. Lev runtime execution should stay behind a Lev-owned entrypoint if it adopts the library.

props.label: Naming Policy
props.title: Lev-Owned Names Avoid Collision
props.subtitle: Use explicit domain nouns in TypeScript packages that may import from effect.

props.label: Proof Design
props.title: Pentagon and UltraQA Anchors
props.subtitle: Promotion requires more than a code example. It must prove multiple substrates.

props.title: Envelope completeness
props.content: Actor, substrate, target, operation, allowed scope, constraints, and evidence are present before ACT.

props.title: Admission correctness
props.content: Unauthorized or underspecified effects do not dispatch.

props.title: Substrate proof
props.content: ObservedEffect extractor returns enough evidence for that substrate.

props.title: Intent preservation
props.content: The observed effect still satisfies the original intent and constraints.

props.title: Override discipline
props.content: Project/user overrides tune policy without silently weakening required core evaluators.

props.title: Hostile cases
props.content: Wrong doc id, browser form submit when only navigation was allowed, shell writes outside cwd, FlowMind overlay removes intent_conservation, semantic pass with no evidence.

props.label: Recommendation
props.title: Adopt EffectEnvelope as the Root Control Primitive
props.subtitle: Do not make dna/ canonical by naming. Do not make boundary universal.

props.content: Make effect the conceptual primitive, expose it as EffectEnvelope and related Lev-owned types, keep boundary as code_boundary, and let FlowMind run the proof loop.

props.title: Next implementation proof
props.content: Prove the contract with three substrates: code edit + code_boundary, document edit + document_scope, and browser or shell action + web/resource effect policy.

props.title: Code
props.content: Target: repo path, package, symbol, file diff. Scope: module/package/API/import ownership. Evaluator: code_boundary.

props.title: Document
props.content: Target: doc id, revision, section/range. Scope: content range, comments, style, sharing policy. Evaluator: document_scope.

props.title: Browser
props.content: Target: origin, tab, session, form, network event. Scope: origin/session/cookie/form-submit authority. Evaluator: web_effect_policy.

props.title: Shell
props.content: Target: cwd, command, process, filesystem. Scope: command allowlist and path/resource scope. Evaluator: resource_effect_policy.

props.title: Cloud/devops
props.content: Target: account, project, resource id. Scope: IAM, environment, region, change class. Evaluator: resource_effect_policy.

props.title: Database
props.content: Target: database, table, row/query/transaction. Scope: schema/table/transaction policy. Evaluator: data_effect_policy.

props.title: Message
props.content: Target: channel, recipient, thread. Scope: audience, send authority, PII/disclosure scope. Evaluator: message_effect_policy.

props.title: Timeline/video
props.content: Target: asset, track, clip, timestamp range. Scope: track/range/export constraints. Evaluator: timeline_effect_scope.

props.title: Graph
props.content: Target: node, edge, claim, projection. Scope: graph operation and projection integrity. Evaluator: graph_projection_integrity.

props.title: FlowMind
props.content: Target: flow id, node, gate, overlay. Scope: declaration schema, version, compatibility. Evaluator: flowmind_declaration_integrity.

props.title: EffectEnvelope
props.content: Runtime wrapper for one agent effect attempt. Avoid a bare Effect export because Effect is already the upstream A/E/R computation type.

props.title: ObservedEffect
props.content: Actual diff, trace, event, revision, or output after ACT. This is substrate evidence, not merely a return value.

props.title: EffectReceipt
props.content: Causal record linking intent, envelope, observed effect, and evaluator decisions.

props.title: EffectPolicy
props.content: Admission and evaluator policy for a class of effects. Keeps policy tied to the side-effect control plane.
